mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term marginal predictive power is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a confirmed economic expansion, a rising index of industrial production reflects accelerating supply-side momentum that translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >50% of expansion-phase observations, or the predictive coefficient flips sign during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260.0, it signals elevated inflation that triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where the index exceeds 260.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises indicates a deteriorating labor market quality or productivity slowdown, which forecasts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a declining industrial production index signals that supply-side contraction is outpacing demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in >60% of tightening-cycle observations, or the predictive relationship vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.
